mission.title: keylime: IMA scalability is currently limited
mission.description: IMA scalability is currently limited. This issue affects 2+ users.

steps.title: Identify keylime feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your keylime deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=keylime
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keylime -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: IMA scalability is currently limited

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant keylime resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
While running scaling experiments in our virtual cluster, we have detected multiple serious problems with the current IMA implementation. We are planning to describe the general problem here, then iss

steps.title: Apply the fix for IMA scalability is currently limited
steps.description:
…t table

Implement an AllowlistDBProxy that acts as a proxy and cache to the
allowlists DB table by holding the VerifierAllowlist DB entries in
a map whose keys are the IDs of the allowlists. Whenever one needs a
VerfierAllowlist DB entry one queries a AllowlistDBProxy singleton for an
entry with that allowlist ID. If the entry is not currently cached in
the map it will be loaded from the DB and otherwise every few seconds the
currently cached entry will be checked for an update and the latest 

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Upgrade keylime to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade keylime:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade keylime jetstack/keylime --namespace cert-manager
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager
helm list -n cert-manager
```

steps.title: Confirm IMA scalability is currently limited is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keylime -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: …t table

Implement an AllowlistDBProxy that acts as a proxy and cache to the
allowlists DB table by holding the VerifierAllowlist DB entries in
a map whose keys are the IDs of the allowlists. Whenever one needs a
VerfierAllowlist DB entry one queries a AllowlistDBProxy singleton for an
entry with that allowlist ID. If the entry is not currently cached in
the map it will be loaded from the DB and . This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with keylime installed or the issue environment reproducible.
